title: Add PWA support and fix live step counter in Chrome

requirements.text: Add full PWA support to the app so it installs and launches correctly from the home screen on Android Chrome. This includes: creating a web app manifest (manifest.json) with correct name, icons, start_url, display mode set to 'standalone', theme_color, and background_color; registering a service worker that caches the app shell for offline/standalone launch; and linking the manifest in index.html. The app must open successfully when launched via a home screen icon added through Chrome's 'Add to Home Screen' flow.

quotes: when opening it by tapping the icon in homescreen it's not
I can update the app to add full PWA support so it installs and launches correctly from your home screen

acceptanceCriteria: A valid manifest.json is served and linked in index.html with name, icons, start_url, display='standalone', theme_color, and background_color fields
A service worker is registered that caches the app shell (HTML, JS, CSS) so the app loads when launched from the home screen
Chrome's 'Add to Home Screen' prompt or menu option successfully adds the app icon to the home screen
Tapping the home screen icon opens the app in standalone mode (no browser chrome/address bar) without a blank screen or error

requirements.text: Fix the live step counter (useStepTracker hook and StepTrackerWidget component) so it correctly detects and counts steps in Chrome on Android. The DeviceMotion API in Chrome requires a permissions prompt (DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission on some devices) and the event listener must be properly registered. Ensure the step detection algorithm works with Chrome's DeviceMotion event data, that permission is requested correctly, and that the live count updates visibly in the StepTrackerWidget UI in real time while walking.

quotes: update the live step counter as it's not working in chrome too

acceptanceCriteria: The step counter requests DeviceMotion permission in Chrome when the user starts tracking, displaying a permission prompt if required
Steps are counted and the count increments in real time on Chrome for Android when the device is moved/walked with
The StepTrackerWidget shows the live step count updating as steps are taken
If permission is denied, a clear message is shown to the user explaining that motion permission is needed for step tracking
The step count persists to localStorage and auto-saves as before

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
PWA service worker must not interfere with Internet Identity authentication redirects or ICP canister calls
Preserve all existing backend data models and API contracts
nonGoals: iOS Safari PWA support is not required for this request
Offline data sync or background sync for fitness/finance data is out of scope
Changes to the backend Motoko actor are not required

required: App icon for PWA manifest — solid dark background with a stylized 'FF' monogram in green and teal gradient, clean modern sans-serif, multiple sizes implied (pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png)
